feature.name: Brain Next Level — 5 Enhancements That Make 60 Feel Alive

stories.title: Create useBrainInsights hook — cross-reference commitments, decay, sentiment
acceptance: New hook src/lib/hooks/useBrainInsights.ts
Queries: overdue commitments from deal_memory_events, contacts with strength < 0.4 from contact_memory, deals with negative sentiment from recent meetings
Cross-references: matches decaying contacts to their deals, finds commitments tied to at-risk deals
Produces 2-3 actionable insight objects with: title, body, urgency, actionUrl, contactName, dealName
Each insight is a complete sentence with context (not just a metric)
Uses React Query with 5-minute stale time
Typecheck passes

stories.title: Build BrainInsightCards component — proactive insights in Brain header
acceptance: New component src/components/brain/BrainInsightCards.tsx
Renders 2-3 insight cards between PipelineSentimentTicker and tabs on BrainPage
Each card: colored left border (amber=action needed, red=urgent, blue=opportunity), icon, title, body text, action button
Action button navigates to relevant page (deal detail, contact, copilot with context)
Cards animate in with subtle fade (no heavy animation)
Dismissable per card (stores dismissed IDs in localStorage, resets daily)
Empty state: hidden entirely (no 'no insights' message)
Wired into BrainPage.tsx between sentiment ticker and tabs
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser on localhost:5175

stories.title: Add relationship_strength_history to contact_memory + snapshot on decay
acceptance: New migration adds strength_history JSONB DEFAULT '[]' to contact_memory
Each entry: { strength: number, date: string, event?: string }
runRelationshipDecay() appends snapshot to strength_history after each decay run
updateContactFromEvent() appends snapshot when strength changes from events
History capped at 30 entries (oldest trimmed on append)
Migration is re-runnable
Typecheck passes

stories.title: Build RelationshipPulseGraph sparkline in Contact Memory tab
acceptance: New component src/components/brain/RelationshipPulseGraph.tsx
Recharts AreaChart sparkline showing strength_history over time
Color gradient: green (>0.7) to amber (0.4-0.7) to red (<0.4)
Hovering a point shows: date, strength %, event that caused the change
Threshold line at 0.4 (dashed red) showing the decay alert boundary
Integrated into BrainContactMemory detail panel replacing or augmenting the static strength bar
Compact size: 100% width, ~120px height
Empty state: shows flat line at current strength if no history
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser on localhost:5175

stories.title: Build MeetingPrepCard — 30-second glanceable prep before meetings
acceptance: New component src/components/brain/MeetingPrepCard.tsx
Queries calendar_events for meetings starting within next 90 minutes for the user
For each upcoming meeting, fetches: contact_memory (strength, style), copilot_memories (recent context), deal_memory_events (open commitments, objections)
Renders a compact card: meeting title, time until ('in 58 min'), contact name + strength bar, 3-4 bullet points (style hint, open commitment, last sentiment, suggested opener)
Suggested opener generated from context (not LLM — template-based from objections/commitments)
Card auto-appears when meeting is within 90 min, auto-hides after meeting start time
Positioned on Brain page between insight cards and tabs (only when upcoming meeting exists)
If no upcoming meetings, component returns null (hidden)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser on localhost:5175

stories.title: Create useDealMomentum hook — composite momentum score per deal
acceptance: New hook src/lib/hooks/useDealMomentum.ts
For each active deal, computes momentum from 4 signals (all SQL, no LLM):
1. Meeting frequency trend: meetings in last 14 days vs prior 14 days
2. Sentiment trajectory: avg sentiment of last 3 meetings vs prior 3
3. Commitment health: fulfilled/total ratio from deal_memory_events
4. Recency: days since last interaction from contact_memory
Momentum score: -1.0 (stalling) to +1.0 (accelerating), computed as weighted average
Status label: 'accelerating' (>0.3), 'steady' (-0.3 to 0.3), 'decelerating' (-0.3 to -0.6), 'stalled' (<-0.6)
Returns array of { dealId, dealName, company, momentum, status, signals }
Uses React Query with 5-minute stale time
Typecheck passes

stories.title: Add momentum indicators to Deal Memory tab + Pipeline Sentiment Ticker
acceptance: Deal Memory tab: deal selector dropdown shows momentum arrow + label next to each deal name
Selected deal shows momentum card at top: score, 4 signal breakdown, trend arrow
PipelineSentimentTicker: each deal row gains momentum arrow (replaces or augments existing trend)
Momentum arrows: green up (accelerating), gray horizontal (steady), amber down (decelerating), red down (stalled)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser on localhost:5175

stories.title: Copilot memory playback — proactive memory recall on deal/contact mention
acceptance: copilot-autonomous detects deal or contact mentions in user message (company name, contact name, deal name)
When detected, queries Brain tables for that entity: contact_memory, recent deal_memory_events, copilot_memories
Prepends a [MEMORY RECALL] block to the system prompt: 'Here is what you remember about {entity}...'
Block includes: relationship strength, meeting count, open commitments, recent objections, last sentiment
LLM naturally incorporates memory into its response (no new structured panel needed)
Max 400 tokens for memory recall block
Only triggers for first message in conversation about an entity (not every turn)
Falls back gracefully if no memory exists (no block added)
Typecheck passes

parallelGroups.reason: Data layer: insights hook, strength history schema, momentum hook — all independent
UI layer: insight cards, pulse graph, meeting prep card, momentum indicators
Copilot integration: memory playback (needs insights hook pattern)
